contraindications.reason: History of hypersensitivity to aztreonam or any component of the formulation
Limited cross-reactivity data; use with caution in patients with history of severe hypersensitivity to other beta-lactams (e.g., penicillins, cephalosporins) due to potential cross-reactivity risk
History of hypersensitivity to penicillins or any component, including immediate-type reactions like urticaria, angioedema, or anaphylaxis
Potential cross-reactivity in patients with a history of severe hypersensitivity to cephalosporins; caution warranted
History of hypersensitivity to vancomycin or any component of the formulation

contraindications.contraindication_name: systemic vancomycin contraindication in C. difficile infection
contraindications.reason: Oral vancomycin must only be used for enteric infections; parenteral vancomycin is not effective for treating C. difficile colitis.
History of hypersensitivity to vancomycin or any component of the formulation
Increased risk of nephrotoxicity and ototoxicity; requires careful dose adjustment and therapeutic drug monitoring
Risk of additive ototoxicity, especially at high doses or with concomitant ototoxic agents
History of hypersensitivity to vancomycin or any component of the formulation
Oral vancomycin is not systemically absorbed; it is not effective for treating systemic infections and must not be used for this purpose
History of hypersensitivity to amikacin, other aminoglycosides, or any component
Pregnancy Category D; can cause fetal harm, including irreversible bilateral congenital deafness, when administered to a pregnant woman
Increased risk of nephrotoxicity and ototoxicity due to reduced drug clearance; requires dose adjustment

contraindications.contraindication_name: pre-existing hearing loss or vestibular dysfunction
contraindications.reason: Risk of additive ototoxicity and vestibulotoxicity
Aminoglycosides can impair neuromuscular transmission and may precipitate or exacerbate muscle weakness
Loop diuretics (e.g., furosemide) may potentiate aminoglycoside ototoxicity
History of hypersensitivity to ampicillin, sulbactam, or any penicillin, including anaphylaxis, angioedema, or severe skin reactions
Ampicillin is associated with a high incidence of non-allergic morbilliform skin rash in patients with Epstein-Barr virus infection
Potential cross-reactivity in patients with a history of severe hypersensitivity to cephalosporins
Sulbactam and ampicillin are renally cleared; dose adjustment required to prevent accumulation and toxicity
History of hypersensitivity to ceftazidime, avibactam, or any component
Ceftazidime, a cephalosporin, may cross-react in patients with a history of immediate-type hypersensitivity reaction (e.g., anaphylaxis) to penicillins
Both components are renally cleared; dose adjustment is required to prevent seizure and other toxicities
History of hypersensitivity to aztreonam or any component
Potential cross-reactivity exists, particularly with ceftazidime due to a shared side chain; caution in severe hypersensitivity to other beta-lactams (except for this specific side chain issue)
History of hypersensitivity to bacitracin or any component
Bacitracin injection is nephrotoxic; contraindicated in patients with pre-existing severe renal disease due to high risk of acute kidney injury
Additive nephrotoxicity risk with other nephrotoxic agents (e.g., aminoglycosides, cisplatin)
History of hypersensitivity to delafloxacin or any fluoroquinolone
Fluoroquinolones may exacerbate muscle weakness in patients with myasthenia gravis, potentially causing life-threatening respiratory depression
Potential risk of arthropathy and tendon injury in weight-bearing joints in children; use reserved for serious infections when no alternatives exist
Limited data; potential risk of fetal cartilage damage based on juvenile animal studies
Increased risk of tendonitis and tendon rupture, particularly in patients >60, on corticosteroids, or with history of tendon disorders

generic_name: penicillin G benzathine and penicillin G procaine

contraindications.reason: History of hypersensitivity to penicillins or components, including procaine
History of hypersensitivity to procaine or other ester-type anesthetics
This formulation is for deep intramuscular injection only; intravenous administration can cause cardiorespiratory arrest and death
Aspirate prior to injection to avoid accidental intravascular delivery, which can cause anaphylactoid reaction to procaine or vascular occlusion by benzathine crystals
History of hypersensitivity to cefotetan or any cephalosporin
Cross-reactivity risk in patients with history of immediate-type hypersensitivity (e.g., anaphylaxis) to penicillins
Cefotetan contains an N-methylthiotetrazole side chain, which can cause hypoprothrombinemia and increase bleeding risk
Potentiation of anticoagulant effects due to hypoprothrombinemia; requires close monitoring of INR
Dose adjustment required based on creatinine clearance to prevent accumulation and neurotoxicity
History of hypersensitivity to ceftolozane, tazobactam, any cephalosporin, or any component
Potential cross-reactivity with cephalosporins in patients with a history of severe hypersensitivity to penicillins
Both components are renally excreted; dose adjustment necessary to avoid accumulation and neurotoxicity
History of hypersensitivity to lefamulin or any components
Lefamulin prolongs the QT interval, which can cause torsades de pointes; contraindicated in patients with known QT prolongation, congenital long QT syndrome, or those on Class IA/III antiarrhythmics

contraindications.contraindication_name: concurrent CYP3A4 inhibitors that prolong QT
contraindications.reason: Concomitant use of strong CYP3A4 inducers or substrates with known risk of QT prolongation is contraindicated due to increased exposure and risk of arrhythmias
Lefamulin primarily metabolized by liver; increased exposure risk in severe hepatic impairment; use with caution
Limited data; potential fetal harm based on animal reproductive studies
History of hypersensitivity to nafcillin, penicillins, or any component
Nafcillin is primarily hepatically cleared; risk of accumulation and worsening of hepatotoxicity in severe liver disease
Caution in patients with history of severe hypersensitivity to cephalosporins due to potential cross-reactivity
History of hypersensitivity to omadacycline, any tetracycline, or any component
Can cause fetal harm including discoloration of deciduous teeth and inhibition of bone growth when administered during the second and third trimester
Risk of permanent tooth discoloration and enamel hypoplasia, and reversible inhibition of bone growth
Limited data; potential for tooth discoloration and bone growth inhibition in the nursing infant
History of hypersensitivity to oxacillin, penicillins, or any component
Oxacillin has been associated with idiosyncratic hepatotoxicity; caution in pre-existing liver disease
Potential cross-reactivity in patients with severe cephalosporin hypersensitivity
History of hypersensitivity to penicillins or any component
Penicillin G potassium contains 1.7 mEq of potassium per million units; high doses may exacerbate hyperkalemia, especially in renal impairment
High potassium load and reduced renal excretion increase risk of hyperkalemia and neurotoxicity
History of hypersensitivity to penicillins or any component

contraindications.contraindication_name: congestive heart failure or sodium-restricted diet
contraindications.reason: High sodium content (2.0 mEq of sodium per million units); use with caution in patients with heart failure, edema, or hypertension
Risk of sodium overload and neurotoxicity due to reduced drug clearance; dose adjustment required
History of hypersensitivity to penicillins, procaine, or any component
History of hypersensitivity to procaine or other ester-type local anesthetics
Must only be administered by deep intramuscular injection; intravascular injection can cause a severe anaphylactoid reaction to procaine and/or vascular occlusion
Procaine penicillin accidental intravascular injection during near-term pregnancy has been associated with maternal and fetal distress; otherwise, indicated for syphilis in pregnancy

Immunosuppressive effect may worsen active infections; must resolve infection before initiating therapy
Screen for TB before initiation; untreated latent TB can reactivate; initiate anti-TB therapy prior to starting ustekinumab
Immunosuppression may increase risk of malignancy; caution in patients with history of cancer
Avoid live vaccines during treatment; BCG vaccines should not be given within one year of initiating ustekinumab

Can impair immune response; risk of worsening infection
Screen for TB; cases of reactivation reported with IL-17 inhibitors; treat latent TB before starting
Secukinumab may exacerbate Crohn's disease; use with caution in inflammatory bowel disease
Avoid live vaccines during therapy

Risk of worsening infection due to immunosuppression

Screen for TB; initiate anti-TB therapy prior to treatment
Avoid live vaccines during treatment

Avoid live vaccines during therapy

contraindications.contraindication_name: moderate to severe heart failure (NYHA Class III/IV)
contraindications.reason: Infliximab doses >5 mg/kg associated with increased mortality and hospitalization due to worsening heart failure
TNF-alpha inhibition can significantly worsen infections and lead to sepsis

Screen for HBV; risk of reactivation; consider antiviral prophylaxis
Avoid live vaccines during therapy
TNF inhibitors may exacerbate or initiate CNS demyelination; avoid in multiple sclerosis
History of hypersensitivity to halobetasol, tazarotene, or any component
Tazarotene is a retinoid and teratogen; must not be used in pregnant women or those planning pregnancy; obtain negative pregnancy test before initiation and ensure effective contraception

contraindications.reason: Corticosteroid and retinoid may exacerbate viral skin infections

contraindications.reason: Can cause severe irritation or increase systemic absorption; avoid use on damaged or eczematous skin
